title: Philanthropy
subtitle: Support the Active Inference Institute as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.
audience: Individuals and organizations interested in donating to support open-science work in Active Inference.
lede: The Active Inference Institute is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Donations from individuals and organizations directly fund public education, research infrastructure, community events, and ecosystem stewardship.

sections.heading: Why Donate
sections.body: The Active Inference Institute operates as an open-science nonprofit. All of our educational materials, research outputs, software, and community resources are freely available to the public. This work requires infrastructure, coordination, and sustained effort that philanthropic support makes possible. Donations help ensure that Active Inference education and research remains accessible to anyone, regardless of institutional affiliation or geographic location.

sections.heading: What Your Donation Supports
sections.body: Philanthropic contributions fund: operational infrastructure for the Institute and its community platforms; audio-visual production for livestreams, podcasts, and educational recordings; event organization for the Applied Active Inference Symposium and other gatherings; educational program development including textbook groups, courses, and workshops; support for interns, fellows, and volunteers; and maintenance of shared computational and storage resources.

sections.heading: Tax Deductibility
sections.body: The Active Inference Institute is incorporated in Delaware, USA as a 501(c)(3) educational nonprofit organization. Donations from US taxpayers are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law. Donors receive a receipt acknowledging their contribution. Please consult your tax advisor for guidance on deductibility in your specific situation.

sections.heading: Organizational Giving
sections.body: Organizations that wish to provide larger gifts or ongoing support may do so through a combination of philanthropic giving and formal partnership. If your organization is interested in providing significant philanthropic support, reach out to discuss how your contribution can best serve the Institute's mission and your organization's goals.

sections.heading: Other Ways to Support
sections.body: Beyond direct donation, you can support the Institute by volunteering your time, contributing to open-source projects, sharing Institute resources with your networks, and participating in community activities. Every form of contribution — time, expertise, and financial — helps sustain the work.

cards.title: 501(c)(3) nonprofit
cards.text: US tax-deductible donations support open-science education and research.

cards.title: Education and events
cards.text: Donations fund courses, textbook groups, symposia, and community infrastructure.

cards.title: Interns and fellows
cards.text: Philanthropic support sustains internship and fellowship programs.

cards.title: Organizational giving
cards.text: Larger gifts and ongoing organizational support through partnership and donation.
